commit.message:
fix(nostr): pass explicit retry options to ndk-blossom uploads (#58)

* fix(nostr): pass explicit retry options to ndk-blossom upload

ndk-blossom v2.0.2 has a bug where uploadToServer passes
options.maxRetries (undefined) to fetchWithRetry, which
spreads over DEFAULT_RETRY_OPTIONS and overrides maxRetries
with undefined. This causes the while loop condition
(attempts <= undefined) to be false, so the fetch never
executes at all.

Fix by explicitly passing maxRetries: 3 and retryDelay: 1000
in the upload options.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: trigger ci

commit.message:
Add get_loc_stats.sh: scalable LOC stats for orgs/enterprises (#7)

* Add get_loc_stats.sh: scalable LOC stats for orgs/enterprises

Collects lines added/deleted per contributor across an entire GitHub
organization or enterprise using batched GraphQL queries. Supports
GitHub CLI and GitHub App authentication modes.

Features:
- Batched GraphQL queries (configurable repos per call) for scalability
- Dual auth: gh CLI (5K req/hr) or GitHub App JWT (15K req/hr)
- Dual scope: --org or --enterprise
- Per-contributor and per-org aggregation in CSV and/or JSON
- Rate-limit aware with automatic sleep/retry
- --test and --max-repos flags for development/testing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Coerce null additions/deletions to 0 via jq (// 0) to prevent null

signature bytes had values > 127.

wrap around (lines 2817, 2426) by widening to uint64_t before addition.

Fix canvas state corruption: try/finally guards on all save/restore

The draw() method uses nested ctx.save()/restore() for screen shake
and camera transforms. If ANY draw sub-method throws an error, the
restore() calls were skipped, permanently corrupting the canvas
transform state (stuck zoomed/translated).

Fix: wrap both save/restore pairs with try/finally to guarantee
restore always runs. Individual draw methods also wrapped in
try/catch to log errors without breaking the chain.

Fix OAuth exchange: persist redirect_uri between start and callback

The redirect_uri built during /oauth/start (from Referer header) differed
from the one built during /oauth/callback (no Referer, different headers).
Google requires the exact same redirect_uri for both auth and exchange.

Fix: save the redirect_uri to DB during /start, reuse it in /callback.
Also: surface actual error messages in the redirect URL for debugging.

commit.message:
Remove Clerk-metadata limit functions — all enforcement via Convex DB

Delete client-side limit helpers (testLimits, spaceLimits,
knowledgePieceLimits, knowledgeLimits, getPlanFromAuth) that derived
plan limits from Clerk JWT privateMetadata. These were either dead
code or created a stale-JWT vulnerability (the test generation bug).

All enforcement now flows uniformly through getAuthedContext →
getEffectiveAccessLevel → subscriptions DB, making the system
consistent across every feature (spaces, tests, deep dives, AI tutor,
answer validation).

Also remove unused plan-level check functions from middleware.ts
(hasValidSubscription, getUserAccessLevel, requirePlanLevel) that
were defined but never called.

commit.message:
Fix game freeze on mouse move  massive performance optimization

Root causes:
1. drawPixelConnection used 3 createLinearGradient passes per connection
   per frame  replaced with simple animated dots (10x faster)
2. Ghost preview recomputed full MST with Prim's algorithm every frame
   on mouse move — replaced with simple nearest-node line
3. Particle drawing used bezier curve trails with per-segment stroke
   calls  replaced with simple fillRect dots
4. Game loop now wrapped in try/catch to prevent silent crashes

8 new security heuristics designed from coverage gap analysis to exercise
previously untouched iccDEV library code paths:

H95: Sparse Matrix Array Bounds Validation (CWE-125/CWE-787)
     Exercises IccSparseMatrix.cpp — GetSparseMatrix, Rows, Cols, MemSize
H96: Embedded Profile Recursion Detection (CWE-674/CWE-400)
     Exercises IccTagEmbedIcc.cpp — GetProfile, recursive nesting check
H97: Profile Sequence Identifier Validation (CWE-125/CWE-400)
     Exercises IccTagProfSeqId.cpp — begin/end iterators, GetFirst/GetLast
H98: Spectral MPE Element Validation (CWE-125/CWE-682)
     Exercises IccMpeSpectral.cpp — SpectralMatrix, SpectralCLUT, Observer
H99: Embedded Height/Normal Image Validation (CWE-120/CWE-787)
     Exercises IccTagEmbedIcc.cpp — embedded image type scanning
H100: Profile Sequence Description Consistency (CWE-125/CWE-120)
      Exercises IccTagBasic.cpp — CIccTagProfileSeqDesc Describe path
H101: MPE Sub-Element Channel Continuity (CWE-125/CWE-787)
      Exercises IccMpeBasic.cpp — full pipeline in→out channel chain
H102: Tag Size vs Profile Size Cross-Check (CWE-125/CWE-120)
      Exercises IccProfile.cpp — m_Tags iteration, offset/size validation

* fix(web): inline pages function wrapper to fix env bindings

CF Pages only injects env bindings for handlers defined
directly in /functions. A bare re-export loses context.env
at runtime, causing secrets to appear missing.

Replace the repo-root re-export shim with an explicit
wrapper that receives and forwards the context object.

Also enrich the error diagnostic payload with envKeyCount
and envKeys so the next deploy reveals exactly what CF
is injecting (or not).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(web): inline root function and add nodejs_compat fallback

secrets into context.env (known platform limitation).

- Inline the full handler at repo-root functions/ instead
  of re-exporting from apps/web/functions/ (re-exports can
  lose env bindings)
- Add process.env fallback via resolveApiKey() for envs
  where nodejs_compat is active
- Add wrangler.toml with nodejs_compat compatibility flag
- Include actionable hint in error response directing user
  to check Preview environment secret configuration

* fix(web): add wrangler project config and source markers

Add pages_build_output_dir and project name to wrangler.toml
so CF Pages properly discovers the functions directory and
wires env bindings.

Add source field to diagnostic error to identify which
functions/ directory CF is actually serving from.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(fleet): add circuit breaker for missing API secret

When the livery API returns a 500 indicating the Gemini
secret is not configured, trip a module-level flag that
prevents all subsequent aircraft from attempting generation.

This avoids error spam in preview deployments where CF
Pages secrets may not be bound to the Preview environment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
